summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the Theme::upload endpoint accepts a crafted ZIP archive with path traversal sequences and writes files outside the intended extraction directory, demonstrating arbitrary file write capability that can lead to RCE.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should upload a malicious theme ZIP containing a file with path traversal sequence (../../../public/test_pwned.php). The PoC should successfully write a PHP file outside the theme directory, demonstrating arbitrary file write capability that can be leveraged for RCE.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built the CI4MS application, uploaded the crafted theme ZIP via the Theme::upload endpoint, and successfully wrote a test PHP file to public/test_pwned.php, confirming the arbitrary file write vulnerability.
verification_method: The run script checks for the existence of the test PHP file (public/test_pwned.php) outside the intended theme directory. The file presence confirms successful path traversal exploitation. The script also verifies the file content matches the expected marker string to distinguish from false positives.
evidence_produced_by_run: The crafted theme ZIP was successfully uploaded via the authenticated Theme::upload endpoint.
A PHP file was created at public/test_pwned.php outside the intended public/templates/ directory.
The file contains the expected marker string, confirming attacker-controlled content was written.
The application logs show the theme upload process completed without errors.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
